text.title: lb-layer-selection Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for lb-layer-selection as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Treating L4 vs L7 as mutually exclusive on a single stack.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Modern architectures use L4 (NLB/HAProxy TCP) in front of L7 (Ingress/Envoy) to get anycast distribution and cross-zone load balancing at L4 while retaining content routing at L7. Collapsing this to a single binary choice misses the hybrid pattern.

failure-mode.description: Quoting sub-second GSLB failover SLOs when using DNS-based routing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DNS TTLs are honored unevenly by clients and intermediary resolvers. Failover after a backend failure takes 30 seconds to 5 minutes in practice. Use anycast or set realistic SLOs that match measured DNS propagation times.
